sourceTitle: Getting started with templates for your team

questions.question: Where can you add a Notion template to your workspace?

options.text: Through the Templates button in the sidebar or when creating a new page
options.explanation: The article states you add templates via the Templates button in the sidebar or during page creation.

options.text: Only by importing a CSV file
options.explanation: CSV import is not mentioned as a way to add templates.

options.text: By typing the word “template” in a page title
options.explanation: Simply naming a page does not add a template.

options.text: Through the Notion mobile app settings
options.explanation: The article does not reference the mobile app settings for adding templates.

questions.question: Which type of Notion template does the guide specifically recommend for teams?

options.text: Database‑powered templates such as To‑do list, Projects & tasks, Meetings, and Docs
options.explanation: The guide highlights these four database‑powered templates as recommended.

options.text: Personal journal templates
options.explanation: Personal journal templates are not listed as a recommendation.

options.text: Markdown import templates
options.explanation: Markdown import is not discussed in the guide.

options.text: Image gallery templates
options.explanation: Image gallery templates are not mentioned.

questions.question: What is a key benefit of database entries in Notion according to the article?

options.text: Each entry is a fully functional Notion page that can host any content type
options.explanation: The article explains that database entries act as full pages capable of containing any Notion content.

options.text: Entries can only store plain text and numbers
options.explanation: Entries can store many property types, not just plain text and numbers.

options.text: They automatically sync with external Google Docs
options.explanation: Automatic syncing with Google Docs is not mentioned.

options.text: They are read‑only once created
options.explanation: Database entries are editable, not read‑only.

questions.question: How do relation properties enhance the functionality of the Projects & tasks template?

options.text: They allow tasks to be linked to their associated projects
options.explanation: Relation properties are used specifically to connect tasks with projects in that template.

options.text: They automatically assign tasks to team members based on workload
options.explanation: Automatic assignment is not described; relation properties only create links.

options.text: They generate a visual Gantt chart for each project
options.explanation: Gantt charts are not mentioned as a result of relation properties.

options.text: They convert tasks into calendar events
options.explanation: Conversion to calendar events is not a function of relation properties.

questions.question: What does the completion property in the Projects template calculate?

options.text: The percentage of tasks marked as Done
options.explanation: The guide states the completion property shows the percent of tasks completed.

options.text: The total number of tasks in the database
options.explanation: It calculates a percentage, not a raw count.

options.text: The average time taken to complete each task
options.explanation: Average time is not mentioned as a calculation.

options.text: The number of team members assigned to the project
options.explanation: Team member count is unrelated to the completion property.

questions.question: If you wanted to explore more than the four recommended templates, where should you go?

options.text: Click “More” under Community at the bottom of the Templates sidebar to open the Notion Template Gallery
options.explanation: The article directs users to the Template Gallery via the “More” link.

options.text: Search the Notion app store
options.explanation: There is no Notion app store mentioned.

options.text: Visit the Notion API documentation
options.explanation: API docs are unrelated to browsing templates.

options.text: Open the Settings > Advanced > Templates section
options.explanation: The guide does not reference such a settings path.

questions.question: Why might a team prefer using a database‑powered template over a static page template?

options.text: Because database entries can be filtered, sorted, and related, providing dynamic views and automation that static pages lack
options.explanation: Database‑powered templates enable rich features like filtering, sorting, relations, and calculated properties, which static pages cannot provide.

options.text: Because they reduce the total number of pages in the workspace, saving storage space
options.explanation: Notion storage is not impacted by using databases versus static pages.

options.text: Because they automatically sync with external project‑management tools
options.explanation: Automatic external sync is not a built‑in benefit of database templates.

options.text: Because they lock the content from further editing, ensuring consistency
options.explanation: Database entries remain editable; they are not locked for consistency.

questions.question: The guide mentions over 10,000 templates in the gallery. What strategic advantage does this breadth give teams when adopting Notion?

options.text: It allows teams to find niche or industry‑specific workflows, reducing the time needed to design custom solutions from scratch
options.explanation: A large variety means teams can locate ready‑made templates that match specific needs, accelerating adoption.

options.text: It guarantees that every template is fully compatible with all third‑party integrations
options.explanation: Compatibility with third‑party tools is not assured by the number of templates.

options.text: It ensures that all templates have built‑in AI assistance
options.explanation: AI assistance is not a feature mentioned for the templates.

options.text: It means that Notion does not require any training for new users
options.explanation: Even with many templates, training may still be necessary to use them effectively.
